description: The information security team has completed a container security audit of the software supply chain platform and produced a findings report at /home/ga/Desktop/security_audit_report.txt. Six security violations were identified across workloads in the 'supply-chain' namespace. Your job as the platform security engineer is to read the audit report, identify the specific workloads with violations, and remediate all findings to bring the namespace into compliance. The report lists vulnerability categories and affected service tier — it does not name specific Kubernetes resources. You must correlate findings to workloads by inspecting the cluster. Login to Rancher at https://localhost (cluster: 'local') with credentials admin / Admin12345678!.

injected_violations: sbom-generator: no resource limits (requests and limits both absent)

verification_criteria.C1: registry-proxy: privileged removed, seccompProfile=RuntimeDefault, resource limits set
verification_criteria.C2: artifact-scanner: no hostPath docker.sock mount, runAsNonRoot=true
verification_criteria.C3: build-agent: SYS_ADMIN capability removed from securityContext
verification_criteria.C4: deploy-controller: no cluster-admin ClusterRoleBinding for its ServiceAccount
